key_event.1818-1827: Independence declared amid caudillo instability and O'Higgins's fall
key_event.1828-1837: Portalian order and 1833 Constitution consolidate centralized state
key_event.1838-1847: Portalian consolidation and Yungay victory strengthen the state
key_event.1848-1857: Portalian state consolidated; 1851 civil war crushed; Civil Code
key_event.1858-1867: Liberal-Conservative fusion, Radical Party founding, secularization
key_event.1868-1877: Liberal reforms constraining executive; franchise expansion
key_event.1878-1887: War of the Pacific victory and nitrate revenue windfall
key_event.1888-1897: Civil War of 1891 and Parliamentary Republic's congressional supremacy
key_event.1898-1907: Nitrate-era labor mobilization met by state massacres
key_event.1908-1917: Labor federation (FOCH) and socialist party consolidate worker mobilization
key_event.1918-1927: 1925 Constitution and Ibáñez centralize state authority
key_event.1928-1937: Ibáñez dictatorship, Depression collapse, then constitutional restoration and Popular Front
key_event.1938-1947: Popular Front governance and CORFO-led state industrialization
key_event.1948-1957: Ley Maldita bans Communists as labor keeps organizing (CUT)
key_event.1958-1967: Frei's "Revolution in Liberty": agrarian reform, peasant unions, copper Chileanization
key_event.1968-1977: 1973 coup crushes society, builds despotic state
key_event.1978-1987: 1983 protest waves after debt-crisis shattered regime legitimacy
key_event.1988-1997: 1988 "No" plebiscite and return to democratic government
key_event.1998-2007: Pinochet's fall, 2005 reforms end authoritarian enclaves
key_event.2008-2017: 2011 student movement and durable protest ecosystem
key_event.2018-2027: 2019 estallido social and two failed constitutional rewrites
